title: The Power of Habits

segments.text: Habits are routines that we perform almost automatically.
Brushing your teeth, checking your phone, taking the same route to work — your brain handles these tasks with very little conscious thought.
This is efficient, but it can also be a trap.
Bad habits form the same way good ones do, through repetition.
The good news is that you can rewire them.
The key is identifying the cue that triggers the habit, then replacing the routine while keeping the same reward.

transcript: Habits are routines that we perform almost automatically. Brushing your teeth, checking your phone, taking the same route to work — your brain handles these tasks with very little conscious thought. This is efficient, but it can also be a trap. Bad habits form the same way good ones do, through repetition. The good news is that you can rewire them. The key is identifying the cue that triggers the habit, then replacing the routine while keeping the same reward.
